_comment: New York State and Local Retirement System (NYSLRS) — ERS and PFRS. Consumer-agnostic data only.
systemName: New York State and Local Retirement System
administeredBy: New York State Office of the State Comptroller — Thomas P. DiNapoli, administrative head and trustee

totalMembers.note: ~1.2M total: 710,000+ current and former state/local employees plus 520,000+ retirees and beneficiaries. Nearly 3,000 participating employers.

ERS.name: Employees' Retirement System
ERS.description: Covers most state and local government employees — general employees, correction officers, court officers, and other non-public-safety classifications

PFRS.name: Police and Fire Retirement System
PFRS.description: Covers police officers and paid firefighters for New York State and municipal employers. ~32,000 active members. Note: no Tier 4 in PFRS.
PFRS.pfrs_tiers_note: PFRS has 5 tiers: Tiers 1, 2, 3, 5, and 6 (no Tier 4 in PFRS).

socialSecurity.note: NYSLRS members participate in Social Security. New York public employees pay into SS. Social Security Fairness Act (Jan 2025) eliminated WEP and GPO — NYSLRS members previously affected by WEP/GPO may receive higher SS benefits.

cola.description: 50% of the CPI increase, with a minimum of 1% and a maximum of 3%, applied to the first $18,000 of the member's annual retirement benefit (Single Life Allowance equivalent).
cola.appliesTo: First $18,000 of annual Single Life Allowance benefit

eligibility.option1: Age 62 or older AND retired for 5 or more years
eligibility.option2: Age 55 or older AND retired for 10 or more years (generally applies to special plan members who can retire at any age after minimum service)
eligibility.option3: Receiving a disability pension for 5 or more years

cola.survivorCola: Surviving spouse receiving a lifetime benefit option gets half the COLA amount the member would have received
cola.colaAnnouncement: COLA effective each September; notice sent to retirees in late September

recentCOLA_fy2025_26.note: 2025-26 COLA is 1.2% (50% of CPI rounded). For a $18,000+ annual benefit: $18 per month / $216 per year maximum.

cola.authority: New York State Retirement and Social Security Law

tier6_contribution_brackets.note: Tier 6 (ERS and PFRS) member contribution rates are income-based, recalculated annually on April 1 using earnings from 2 State fiscal years prior. New members: rate based on projected annualized wage for first 3 SFYs.

brackets.note: Up to NY Governor's salary cap (~$250,000)

tier6_contribution_brackets.governorSalaryCapNote: Pensionable earnings may not exceed the salary of the NY Governor (currently $250,000). Contributions and benefits not calculated on earnings above this cap.
tier6_contribution_brackets.updateCycle: Annual — new rates effective April 1 each year

tier_1.note: Legacy tier — very few active members. Most benefits mirror Tier 2.

memberContributions.note: Tier 1 members generally not required to contribute; may make voluntary contributions.

tier_2.membershipDates: July 27, 1976 through August 31, 1983 (Note: Tier 3 spans July 27, 1976 – August 31, 1983 for ERS; see Tier 3 for overlap)
tier_2.note: Legacy tier. Most members are in plans covered under Articles 14 or 15.

memberContributions.note: Tier 2 ERS members generally not required to contribute; may make voluntary contributions.

memberContributions.duration: Until 10 years of membership OR 10 years of credited service — whichever occurs first
memberContributions.note: 3% contributions required until reaching the 10-year threshold, then contributions stop.

earlyRetirementReduction.at55: 27% permanent reduction (with less than 30 years of service)
earlyRetirementReduction.note: See full reduction chart at osc.ny.gov. Reduction is prorated by exact age.

finalAverageEarnings.limitation: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 10%

under20years.structure: 1.66% x FAE x years of service

20to30years.structure: 2% x FAE x years of service

over30years.structure: 2% x FAE x 30 years + 1.5% x FAE x years beyond 30

benefitFormula.note: Article 15 (Coordinated Plan) covers most ERS Tier 3/4 members. Other plans available for specific job titles.

memberContributions.exceptions: NY State Correction Officers not required to contribute after 30 years. Uniformed Court/Peace Officers (UCS) contribute 4% for all years.

retirementAge.noReductionException: UCPOs with at least 30 years of service

earlyRetirementReduction.note: See full reduction chart at osc.ny.gov.

finalAverageEarnings.limitation: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 10%
finalAverageEarnings.overtimeLimit: Overtime above limit excluded from FAE; limit increases 3% per calendar year

under20years.structure: 1.66% x FAE x years of service

20to30years.structure: 2% x FAE x years of service

over30years.structure: 2% x FAE x 30 years + 1.5% x FAE x years beyond 30

vesting.note: Vesting at 5 years established April 9, 2022 law (previously 10 years).

memberContributions.duration: For entire career (except Correction Officers after 30 years)

retirementAge.vestedMemberNote: Vested members who left payroll before retirement eligible at 55; full benefit at 63.

earlyRetirementReduction.note: See full reduction chart at osc.ny.gov. Significantly steeper than earlier tiers.

finalAverageEarnings.limitation: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 10%
finalAverageEarnings.overtimeLimit: Overtime above annual limit excluded; limit adjusts annually by CPI as of September 30 prior year
finalAverageEarnings.additionalExclusions: Lump sum vacation payments, earnings above Governor's salary (~$250K), earnings above primary+secondary employer
finalAverageEarnings.tier6FAEUpdateNote: Effective April 20, 2024: FAE changed from 5 consecutive years to 3 consecutive years (2024 law). Applies to members retiring on or after April 20, 2024.

under20years.structure: 1.66% x FAE x years of service

exactly20years.structure: 1.75% x FAE x 20 years = 35% of FAE

over20years.structure: 1.75% x FAE x 20 years + 2% x FAE x years beyond 20

article15_coordinated.note: Tier 6 uses 1.75% multiplier for first 20 years (slightly above 1.66% of other tiers) but no 2% multiplier until 20+ years is reached.

pfrs.note: PFRS has 5 tiers (1, 2, 3, 5, 6 — no Tier 4). ~98% of PFRS members are in special 20- or 25-year plans. Tier 2 is largest (~55% of PFRS members). Tier 6 is growing (~45%). Tiers 1 and 3 are legacy/small.

specialPlans.overview: Most PFRS members are in special retirement plans under Sections 384, 384-d, 384-e, or 384-f. These allow retirement after 20 or 25 years of creditable service, regardless of age.

section384_25year.name: 25-Year Benefit (Section 384)
section384_25year.additionalYears: 1.66% x FAE per year beyond 25, maximum 61.67% of FAE (32 years max)

section384d_20year.name: 20-Year Benefit (Section 384-d)
section384d_20year.additionalYears: 1.66% x FAE per year beyond 20, maximum 70% of FAE (32 years max)

memberContributions.note: Tier 2: None if employer offers a non-contributory plan. Tier 1: not required to contribute. Both may make voluntary contributions.

retirementAge.specialPlan: After completing minimum service (20 or 25 years), regardless of age
retirementAge.regularPlan: Full benefits at 62; reduced benefits from age 55-62

finalAverageEarnings.limitation: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 20%

tier_1_2.benefitFormula: Special 20/25-year plans dominate. See specialPlans above.

tier_3.note: Very small tier (6-month window). Covered by Article 11 or Article 14 of RSSL.

memberContributions.article11: None if employer offers non-contributory plan
memberContributions.article14: 3% for 25 years or until retirement, whichever is earlier

limitation.article11: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 20%
limitation.article14: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 10%

memberContributions.duration: Until retirement, unless plan limits creditable service to 20, 25, or 32 years (then contributions stop at the maximum)
memberContributions.collectiveBargainingException: Members covered by union-negotiated CBA requiring non-contributory plan (if in effect Jan 9, 2010) may not be required to contribute

retirementAge.specialPlan: After completing minimum service (20 or 25 years), regardless of age
retirementAge.regularPlan: Full benefits at 62; reduced benefits from 55-62

finalAverageEarnings.limitation: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 20%
finalAverageEarnings.overtimeLimit: Overtime limited to 15% of calendar-year earnings per year

tier_6.note: ~45% of PFRS members as of 2023. Fastest-growing tier.

memberContributions.brackets: See tier6_contribution_brackets at top level — same brackets as ERS Tier 6
memberContributions.duration: Until retirement unless plan limits creditable service (20, 25, or 32 years)
memberContributions.collectiveBargainingException: Same as Tier 5 exception if CBA was in effect Jan 9, 2010

retirementAge.specialPlan: After completing minimum service (20 or 25 years), regardless of age
retirementAge.regularPlan: Full benefits at 62 (63 for vested members not on payroll); reduced benefits from 55-62
retirementAge.note: PFRS Tier 6 regular plan full retirement age is 62 (not 63 as in ERS Tier 6)

earlyRetirementReduction.regularPlan: Prorated reduction; at age 55 approximately 27%. See full chart at osc.ny.gov.

finalAverageEarnings.limitation: Earnings in any year cannot exceed average of previous 2 years by more than 10%
finalAverageEarnings.overtimeLimit: Overtime limited to 15% of calendar-year earnings per year
finalAverageEarnings.additionalExclusions: Lump sum vacation payments, earnings above Governor's salary, earnings from more than primary+secondary employers
finalAverageEarnings.tier6FAEUpdateNote: Effective April 1, 2024: FAE changed from 5 consecutive years to 3 consecutive years. Applies to PFRS Tier 6 members retiring on or after April 1, 2024.

benefitFormula.specialPlans: Same as PFRS Tier 2 special plans — 50% of FAE at 20 or 25 years with 1.66% per additional year. See specialPlans above.
benefitFormula.regularPlan: Follows Article 15 or comparable plan provision for regular retirement.

employerContributions.note: Employer average contribution rates for SFY 2025-26: ERS 16.5% of payroll (up from 15.2%); PFRS 33.7% (up from 31.2%). Rates vary by employer based on plan types, salary levels, and member tier distribution. Determined annually by Comptroller based on investment performance and actuarial assumptions.

vesting.note: All tiers (ERS and PFRS): 5 years of credited service required for vested benefit. Law change effective April 9, 2022 reduced Tier 6 vesting from 10 to 5 years. Tiers 1-5 were already 5-year vesting.
vesting.withdrawalOption: Members with less than 10 years credited service may withdraw accumulated contributions with 5% compound interest if they end membership

researchGaps: Full early retirement reduction tables by tier and age (available at osc.ny.gov/retirement/members/about-benefit-reductions)
Special plan details for specific job titles (correction officers, sheriffs, court officers — different formulas)
